Mads Toftrup

I’m a PhD candidate in the Algorithms, Data Structures and Foundations of Machine Learning research group, advised by Prof. Chris Schwiegelshohn at Aarhus University (Department of Computer Science). My expected graduation date is August 2026.

I’m looking for 2026 spring/summer internships

Interests/Experience

I study efficient learning at scale. I’m particularly interested in how we can get rid of the full-precision weights in quantization-aware training of neural networks. This enables us to reap the memory benefits of low-precision formats not just during inference but also during training. A more comprehensive list of topics that interest me includes: quantization of LLMs, second-order optimizers, activation compression, data selection, distillation, and speculative decoding.

Previously, I’ve worked as a student programmer at Danske Bank on fraud detection systems, and as a junior trading analyst at Danske Commodities.

Publications

  • LoQT: Low-Rank Adapters for Quantized Training: S. Loeschcke*, M. Toftrup*, M.J. Kastoryano, S. Belongie, and V. Snæbjarnarson. NeurIPS 2024.

  • †On Generalization Bounds for Projective Clustering: M.S. Bucarelli, M.F. Larsen, C. Schwiegelshohn, and M. Toftrup. NeurIPS 2023.

  • †The Power of Uniform Sampling for Coresets: V. Braverman, V. Cohen-Addad, S.H. Jiang, R. Krauthgamer, C. Schwiegelshohn, M. Toftrup, and X. Wu. FOCS 2022.

  • A reproduction of Apple’s bi-directional LSTM models for language identification in short strings: M. Toftrup, S.A. Sørensen, M.R. Ciosici, and I. Assent. EACL 2021.

*denotes equal contribution/joint first authorship. †Alphabetical ordering

Achievements and awards

  • Best Paper Award, Want@ICML24, 2024. Awarded for our work on quantized pre-training of LLMs without storing high-precision master weights, leading to a 59% memory reduction with no loss in quality.

  • EuroHPC Development Access Grant, 2024. Awarded 3200 A100 GPU hours on MeluXina GPU for distributed LoQT development.

  • Bachelor’s Degree with Distinction, 2018-2021. Graduated with distinction for completing Aarhus University’s Computer Science Talent Program.
